column9: The element does not include ARIA roles necessary to communicate the relationship to the screen reader with secondary elements.
column10: The element includes ARIA roles necessary to communicate the relationship to the screen reader with secondary elements.
column16: Observation: This issue is observed with the similar elements throughout the application.
column18: Remove the role="tab" from the different elements.

column7: ID attribute value must be unique
column8:
1. Go to the URL.
2. Open the Browser Console.
3. Run aXe scan.
4. Look for issue "ID attribute value must be unique" as it's displayed on aXe results section and select "Inspect" button to be taken to the block of code where the issue is located.
column9:
More than one instance of an active ID is found.

Duplicate IDs found:
"module-1626923406783-964940- "

The value assigned to active ID attributes on focusable elements must be unique to prevent the second instance from being overlooked by assistive technology.
column10: No repeated IDs are found.
column16: Observation: This issue is observed on all webpages throughout the application.
column18:
Avoid having two active IDs of a different version (web mobile or desktop version) for the same element at the same time. 
In case the mobile responsive view is displaying, then the only ID that should be active is the ID of the corresponding mobile version. 
Make sure that each focusable element on the page with an active ID has a unique value.

column8:
1. Go to the URL.
2. Navigate through the website using keyboard navigation or tab navigation until reaching links "Live coverage is available, Player is in upcoming featured group's icon, Video highlights available for that player, etc." in the leaderboard table.
3. Notice the purpose of this link is not being correctly communicated to screen reader users.
column9:
The links "Live coverage is available, Player is in upcoming featured group's icon, Video highlights available for that player, etc." in the leaderboard table is not descriptive or accurate, neither is it providing accurate information about where it is redirecting the user to.
 
This might be causing some difficulties for all users to navigate the websiteùbut particularly for those with cognitive, language, and learning disabilities.
column10: The text within the link or the adjacent text provide full context about where the user is going to be redirected to.

column7: All page content must be contained by landmarks
column8:
1. Go to the URL.
2. Run NVDA tool.
3. Press (insert + f7) key and click on landmarks.
4. Notice that HTML document does not contain any landmark.
column9:
The HTML document does not contain any landmark.

Landmarks helps user to understand the structure of the document and to identify piece of content.
column10:
All web pages should have, at least, the following landmarks:
- Header
- Main
- Footer
column16: Observation: This issue is reproducible for all webpages present in the application.
column18:
Use <header> tag as a container of the banner region, <main> tag as a container of main region and <footer> tag as a container of the content info region.

You can also use aria roles for each landmark:
- Header landmark: role=ôbannerö,
- Main landmark: role=ômainö,
- Footer landmark: role=ôcontentinfoö,
- Navigation landmark: role=ônavigationö,
- Search: role=ôsearchö

Finally, ensure that <header>, <main>, <footer> should be a direct child of <body> tag.

Refer to:
https://www.w3.org/WAI/tutorials/page-structure/regions/

column7: Frames must have a title attribute
column8:
1. Go to the URL.
2. Open the Browser Console.
3. Run aXe scan.
4. Look for issue "Frames must have a title attribute" as it's displayed on aXe results section and select "Inspect" button to be taken to the block of code where the issue is located.
column9:
The screen reader does not announce the iframe title.

Screen reader users have the option to pull up a list of titles for all frames on a page. Adding descriptive, unique titles allows users to quickly find the frame they need. If titles are not present, navigating through frames quickly becomes difficult and confusing.
column10: Every frame as a unique title.
column16: Observation: This issue is reproducible for all advertisement frames present in the application.
column18:
1. Use the title attribute to define an accessible name for an iframe element.
2. Use the aria-label attribute to define an accessible name for an iframe element.
3. Use the aria-labelledby attribute to define an accessible name for an iframe element.
4. Accessible names should be short and describe the contents of the iframe element to help users decide whether to navigate to the iframe or not.
5. For iframes with no visible content, explicitly identify the iframe as having no information for the user (e.g. title="No content").

Refer to:
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/ARIA_Techniques/Using_the_aria-label_attribute

column18:
To achieve the minimum contrast ratio between text and an irregular background you can use one of the following techniques:
 
1. Use a background color for the text to pass the minimum contrast ratio, this is achieved by adding a span tag and a class with a background color attribute.
2. Add a <div> tag with a background-color property to pass the contrast ratio and give it a transparency.
3. Add a border to the text which passes the minimum contrast ratio.
4. Add a background gradient that has enough contrast with the text to the <div> container to pass the minimum ratio and place the text over the darkest part of the gradient.

Refer to:
https://www.essentialaccessibility.com/blog/content-over-images-how-does-this-uxui-trend-impact-accessibility/

column18:
This issue could be a result of combining different coding languages, plugins or other embedded applications and it can be avoided by using one of the following mechanisms to provide a way for users to escape the subset of the content:

a. Ensuring that the keyboard function for advancing focus within content (commonly the TAB key) exits the subset of the content after it reaches the final navigation location.

b. Providing a keyboard function to move focus out of the subset of the content. Make sure to document the feature in an accessible manner within the subset.

c. If the technology used in the subset of the content natively provides a "move to parent" keyboard command, then documenting that command before the user enters the plug-in so they know how to get out again.

Refer to:
https://www.w3.org/TR/UNDERSTANDING-WCAG20/keyboard-operation-trapping.html

column16: 1.PGA Tour- Player Details:  A similar issue is observed with  tab "Season, Career, Tournament, Majors" under heading "Performance"
column18:
1. Add role="tablist" and aria-label attribute to the tabs container.
2. The tabs should be a <button> tag or have role="button" and type="button" properties.
3. Each tab must have the following properties:
       role="tab"
       aria-selected="true/false". The first tab should have this value set to "true".
       aria-controls="ID_TABPANEL"
       id="ID_TAB"
4. While having the aria-selected attribute set to none, the button must have an tabindex attribute set to "-1"
5. For the tabpanel use the following attributes:
      tabindex="0"
       role="tabpanel"
       id="ID_TABPANEL"
       aria-labelledby="ID_TAB"
6. Use JS to add LEFT and RIGHT arrow keys interaction to navigate through the tabs

Refer to:
https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria-practices/examples/tabs/tabs-2/tabs.html

column7: Visually appearing heading not tagged as heading
column8:

2. Navigate through the website until reaching text "About Us".
3. Notice that the screen reader announces it as plain text.
column9:
The screen reader announces text "About Us" as plain text.

This could potentially confuse the user since headings are designed to structure the information in the document.
column10:
The headings should be tagged in the below mentioned heading level:
1. Heading level 1: About us
2. Heading level 2: Mission, Headquarters, Overview, Eligibility, Membership, Impact, ..........etc

column18:

https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Techniques/general/G141

column8:
1. Go to the URL.
2. Navigate through the page using the keyboard until you reach the text "fedexcup standings, top 10 finishes, etc".
3. Use the ENTER or SPACEBAR keys to activate the tooltip.
column9:
The tooltip is being displayed only on hover.

This might result in some extra information or hint being unavailable for keyboard-only users.
column10: The tooltip is being displayed whether the element is on hover or on focus status. It can be dismissed by pressing the ESC key and focus goes back to the element which originally opened the tooltip once it's closed.
column18:
1. Wrap the dialog inside a <button> and give a "display:none" to the hidden elements on the page using CSS instead of using the "visibility" property set as "hidden".
2. Provide an attribute type="button" to the <button> element.
3. The dialog should have a tabindex=-1 attribute and it should gain focus when the button is clicked on by using JS method focus().
4. Provide a role="status" so the screen readers can make the announcement.

Refer to:
https://inclusive-components.design/tooltips-toggletips/ 
https://developers.google.com/web/fundamentals/accessibility/focus/using-tabindex
https://css-tricks.com/inclusively-hidden/

2. Set the Scale to 100% and Display Resolution to 1366x768.
3. Open the URL in Chrome.
4. Press the CTRL and + keys at the same time.
5. Increase the browser zoom to 200%.
6. Search for main content and notice that frame "Fedexcup Standings"   is not visible.
column9: Frame "Fedexcup standings" goes beyond the user's visual range and it's rendered incomprehensible because the context is lost.
column10: The content adapts itself to the current viewport and has a logic sequence with the context around it without losing coherence.
column18:
1. Check the display properties and the DOM structure.
2. Use the CSS media query to adjust the properties when zooming the browser to 200%. 

Also ensure that the height and width of content containers are specified using dynamic units like '%', 'em', 'vh' and 'vw'.

Refer to:
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@media
https://www.w3.org/Style/Examples/007/units.en.html

column7: Focus is not visible on elements
column8:
1. Go to the URL.
2. Navigate the page using the keyboard.
column9:
Focus is not visible on combobox "Season ,Time Period, and Tournament" while the user is navigating through focusable elements with the keyboard.

Having a visual reference as to where the user is positioned on the page is important to make navigation easier for keyboard-only users and people with cognitive disabilities or attention disorders.
column10: Focus border is visible around all the focusable elements.
column16: A similar issue is observed with the similar elements throughout the application.
column18:
Provide a visible border/outline on each interactive element using CSS.

Refer to:
https://www.w3.org/TR/UNDERSTANDING-WCAG20/navigation-mechanisms-focus-visible.html
https://www.w3.org/TR/2016/NOTE-WCAG20-TECHS-20161007/C15

column7: ARIA input fields must have an accessible name
column8:
1. Go to the URL.
2. Open the Browser Console.
3. Run aXe scan.
4. Look for the issue"ARIA input fields must have an accessible name" as it is displayed on aXe results section and select "Inspect" button to be taken to the block of code where the issue is located.
column9: The aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty.
column10: Ensure every ARIA input field has an accessible name.
column18:
Use a <select> tag and associate it with its <label>. This tag will also announce the state of the element (expanded/collapsed) without the need of ARIA attributes.

Refer to:
https://www.w3schools.com/tags/tag_select.asp

column8:

3. Notice when reaching elements "don't miss this play now dominate your season... log in ,about ...etc", focus is not fully visible.
column9:
Focus is not fully visible on the element when the user navigates through the focusable elements with the keyboard.

Having a visual reference as to where the user is positioned on the page is important to make navigation easier for keyboard-only users and people with cognitive disabilities or attention disorders.
column10:
Focus border is visible around all the focusable elements.

The color contrast ratio of the focus border against the element's background should be of at least 4.5:1.
column18:
Provide a visible border on each interactive element using CSS. Review absolute values such as "top", "right" to make sure focus is fully visible.

Refer to:
https://www.w3.org/TR/UNDERSTANDING-WCAG20/navigation-mechanisms-focus-visible.html
https://www.w3.org/TR/2016/NOTE-WCAG20-TECHS-20161007/C15

 Add a role="button". Also, make sure the element can be triggered when using the SPACE bar or ENTER key. 

Refer to: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Roles/button_role

column8:
1. Go to the URL.
2. Navigate through the website until reaching the "Search" button.
3. Notice that the screen reader does not announce the current state of the "Search" button.
column9:
The state of the "Search", whether its content is collapsed or expanded, is not announced by the screen reader.

This might confuse the user due to the lack of information they have about the state of the element.
column10: There is an aria-expanded attribute that indicates the current state of the button.
column18:
Use an aria-expanded attribute to provide information about the current state of the element. Initially, set the value to "false" and handle the functionality using JS.

Refer to:
https://www.w3.org/WAI/GL/wiki/Using_aria-expanded_to_indicate_the_state_of_a_collapsible_element

column10: There is an aria-disabled attribute that indicates the current state of the button.
column16: 1. PGA Tour û News: Tuesday july 27,2021 news "previous" disabled button not announced.

column7: Screen reader does not automatically reads the typeahead search results
column8:
1. Go to the URL
2. Navigate through the website until reaching the "Search" button > press enter key "Search" edit field present within header.
3. Notice that screen reader does not read the search results automatically.
column9: Screen reader does not automatically reads the typeahead search results
column10: Screen reader should automatically read the typeahead search results
column18:
Use aria-describedby (see Adding descriptions to elements using aria-describedby), giving a clue that the element provides suggestions upon entering text, and how many options there are available.
Use aria-expanded attribute (see Marking elements expandable using aria-expanded), giving a clue that there is something to be expanded (the suggestions).
Use autocomplete="off" attribute so it does not trigger the browser's autocomplete feature (which remembers previous user input and offers it again).
The suggestions appear upon pressing Up/Down, Esc, or upon a first character is entered into the filter input.
The number of available options is always announced by the screen reader.
This is done using role="alert".

Refer to: https://www.accessibility-developer-guide.com/examples/widgets/autosuggest/

3. Notice the purpose of this link is not being correctly communicated to screen reader users.
column9:
The link "view all" is not descriptive or accurate, neither is it providing accurate information about where it is redirecting the user to.
 
This might be causing some difficulties for all users to navigate the websiteùbut particularly for those with cognitive, language, and learning disabilities.
column10: The text within the link or the adjacent text provide full context about where the user is going to be redirected to.
column16: 1. Tournament Tickets : similar issue found for "buy ticket" link.
column18:
Three possible actions
a. Add more descriptive text within the <a> tag.
b. Use an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute to provide proper context to each link.
c. Use an aria-describedby attribute to add more information about the link.

Refer to:
https://developers.google.com/web/fundamentals/accessibility/semantics-aria/aria-labels-and-relationships
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/ARIA_Techniques/Using_the_aria-describedby_attribute

column8:
1. Go to the URL.
2. Use the Heading Map Chrome extension.
3. Notice there is no level 1 heading.
column9:
There's no heading level 1 that allows screen reader users to skim through the website.

If there is no heading defined on the page, users waste valuable time getting announced the page as a whole to understand its structure.
column10: There is a heading level 1 present on the website.
column18:
Add an <h1> tag or use a role="heading" with an aria-level="1" attribute to identify the main title of the page.

Download the extension on the link below:
https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/headings map/flbjommegcjonpdmenkdiocclhjacmbi/related

Refer to:
https://www.w3.org/WAI/tutorials/page-structure/headings/

column18:

E.g. <h1>, <h2> ... <h6>.

Refer to:
https://www.w3.org/WAI/tutorials/page-structure/headings/

column18:
Error msg not associated
1. Add a unique id="my_unique_id" attribute to each of the elements that contains the error message.
2. Add an aria-describedby="my_unique_id" to each of the form field.
3. Add an aria-invalid="true" to the form element when the form field contains an error.
4. Set focus on the first form field with error using JS.

Refer to:
https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG20-TECHS/ARIA1.html
https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Techniques/aria/ARIA21.html

Add role="alert" attribute to the <element> tag that contains the error message.

Refer to:
https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG20-TECHS/ARIA19.html

column8:
1. Go to the URL.
2. Navigate through the page using the keyboard until you reach the Main content.
3. Notice that tabs "Trending, Competition, Features, Shows " is not triggerable using the keyboard.
column9:
The tabs cannot be activated using keyboard-only navigation.

This is important for users with motor impairments who navigate through the page only using the keyboard.
column10:
Focus reaches the first tab elements while using TAB key. The next focusable element using TAB should be the tabpanel, not the other tabs.
Using the LEFT and RIGHT arrow keys should help the user to navigate through the different tabs.
ENTER key or SPACEbar key should activate the tab.
column18:
1. Add role="tablist" and aria-label attribute to the tabs container.
2. The tabs should be a <button> tag or have role="button" and type="button" properties.
3. Each tab must have the following properties:
       role="tab"
       aria-selected="true/false". The first tab should have this value set to "true".
       aria-controls="ID_TABPANEL"
       id="ID_TAB"
4. While having the aria-selected attribute set to none, the button must have an tabindex attribute set to "-1"
5. For the tabpanel use the following attributes:
      tabindex="0"
       role="tabpanel"
       id="ID_TABPANEL"
       aria-labelledby="ID_TAB"
6. Use JS to add LEFT and RIGHT arrow keys interaction to navigate through the tabs

Refer to:
https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria-practices/examples/tabs/tabs-2/tabs.html

column18:
1. Add role="tablist" and aria-label attribute to the tabs container.
2. The tabs should be a <button> tag or have role="button" and type="button" properties.
3. Each tab must have the following properties:
       role="tab"
       aria-selected="true/false". The first tab should have this value set to "true".
       aria-controls="ID_TABPANEL"
       id="ID_TAB"
4. While having the aria-selected attribute set to none, the button must have an tabindex attribute set to "-1"
5. For the tabpanel use the following attributes:
      tabindex="0"
       role="tabpanel"
       id="ID_TABPANEL"
       aria-labelledby="ID_TAB"
6. Use JS to add LEFT and RIGHT arrow keys interaction to navigate through the tabs

Refer to:
https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria-practices/examples/tabs/tabs-2/tabs.html

https://www.w3.org/WAI/tutorials/images/decorative/

column9:

This might confuse the user due to the lack of information they have about the state of the element.

column18:

https://www.w3.org/WAI/GL/wiki/Using_aria-expanded_to_indicate_the_state_of_a_collapsible_element
